1.description: The cost of capital for banks. This figure shows the equity, debt, and total cost of capital for banks. The equity cost of capital is calculated using the ICC estimate based on analyst earnings forecasts. The debt cost of capital is based on the individual bank's interest payments over the coming year. The total cost of capital is the weighted average of the equity and debt cost of capital weighted by the size of equity and debt. The mean estimate is across all banks in a given month. The figure also shows the 10% and 90% percentiles in the monthly distribution. The cost of capital is measured in percentage points. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

2.description: Slope coefficients from return regressions. This figure shows the slope coefficients from a return regression using realized buy-and-hold returns as a function of the expected return proxy,BHi,t,T=αtT+βT×((1+ICCi,t)T/12−1)+εi,t$BH_{i,t,T} = \alpha ^T_{t} + \beta ^T \times ((1+ICC_{i,t})^{T/12}-1) + \epsilon _{i,t}$, whereBHi,t,T$BH_{i,t,T}$is the buy-and-hold return for bankibetween timetandT, the ICC measure is formed at timet, and the buy-and-hold return therefore corresponds to a horizon ofTmonths. We adjust for delisting and calculate buy-and-hold returns as in Shumway (1997). The graph shows the coefficientβT$\beta ^T$for each prediction horizonTtogether with a 95% confidence band. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

3.description: The capital structure of banks. This figure shows the capital structure for banks. The graphs corresponds the Tier 1 capital ratio, the Tier 2 capital ratio, deposits to total assets, and equity to total assets. The mean estimate in each figure is across all banks in the given year. The figures also show the 10% and 90% percentiles in the monthly distribution of each ratio. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]

4.description: The tax shield ratio for banks. This figure shows the calculated tax shield ratio for banks. The tax shield is calculated using the individual bank's interest payments and a federal tax rate. The present value of the tax shield is found by discounting interest payments with the total cost of capital. The tax shield ratio is the tax shield to total assets. The mean estimate is across all banks in the given month. The figure also shows the 10% and 90% percentiles in the monthly distribution. [Color figure can be viewed atwileyonlinelibrary.com]
